notes: Dry-run evals. Each eval first builds a throwaway git fixture with setup_sandbox.sh (seeded tags + conventional commits + a file:// remote), then a subagent is asked to 'release a new version' and must produce a release PLAN only — no mutating git/gh commands. Assertions check version inference and local-state handling, not real execution.

evals.expected_output: Proposes v1.2.4 (only fix/docs since v1.2.3); clean tree; plan tags v1.2.4 and pushes.

assertions.text: Bump reason cites patch-level commits (fix/docs), no feat

evals.expected_output: Proposes v1.3.0 (a feat: since v1.2.3); clean tree; plan tags v1.3.0 and pushes.

assertions.text: Bump reason cites a feat: commit
Bump reason cites a breaking change

evals.expected_output: Detects uncommitted changes and ASKS the user whether to include them before doing anything else.

assertions.text: Asks the user whether to commit/include the changes (does not auto-commit or ignore)

evals.expected_output: Detects local-only commits and pushes them (and opens a PR for a feature branch) before tagging.

assertions.text: Plans to push first before tagging

evals.expected_output: Warns there is nothing to release (no commits since the last tag).

assertions.text: Warns that there are no commits since the last tag
